description: Machine-readable index of all significant files in the Thriving Neighborhoods pillar repository.
research_corpus: See research/index.json for individual research report entries.

files.title: Thriving Neighborhoods — Decision Funnel
files.summary: Primary entry point and decision funnel for hackathon teams. Contains repo map, decision phases, guardrails, and Hackbot resources.

files.title: Quick Start Guide
files.summary: Fast-track orientation for new teams — what to read first, how to get moving in the first 30 minutes.

files.title: Hackbot Agent Specification
files.summary: Full specification for the Hackbot AI agent, including boot sequence, skills, research corpus navigation, and guardrails.

files.title: Claude Code Configuration
files.summary: Configuration and behavioral rules for Claude Code when operating in this repository.

files.title: Research Corpus Guide
files.summary: Canonical orientation document for navigating the research corpus, including source-of-truth hierarchy, section structure, navigation decision tree, and agent behavior rules.

files.title: Maintenance Guide
files.summary: Instructions for adding, updating, and synchronizing research reports and metadata files to keep the corpus consistent and agent-navigable.

files.title: Hackbot Boot Prompt
files.summary: System prompt used to initialize the Hackbot AI assistant at the start of a session.

files.title: Evidence Log — Thriving Neighborhoods
files.summary: Tracks verified and unverified factual claims used in research and demos, with official source URLs and verification status.

files.title: Pillar Overview
files.summary: Overview of the Thriving Neighborhoods pillar, its goals, problem areas, and how it relates to the broader Richmond civic hackathon MAP framework.

files.title: MAP Alignment
files.summary: How the Thriving Neighborhoods pillar aligns with the MAP framework, including strategic priorities and evaluation dimensions.

files.title: Blue Sky Problem Statements
files.summary: Broad, aspirational problem statements for the Thriving Neighborhoods pillar before rubric scoring and scoping.

files.title: Targeted Problem Statements (Scored)
files.summary: Scored and ranked problem statements for the Thriving Neighborhoods pillar, including rubric evaluation for development discovery (26/32) and housing compliance (22/32).

files.title: Data Source Index
files.summary: Index of all data sources documented for the Thriving Neighborhoods pillar, with access methods and key fields.

files.title: Source Inventory (CSV)
files.summary: Machine-readable inventory of all data sources with access mode, key fields, and update cadence.

files.title: User Journeys
files.summary: Synthesized user journey maps for key Thriving Neighborhoods user personas, grounded in research findings.

files.title: Housing Data Feasibility
files.summary: Assessment of the feasibility of using available housing data for hackathon builds, including known gaps and recommended workarounds.

files.title: Benchmark Scan
files.summary: Scan of comparable civic tech tools from other cities relevant to the Thriving Neighborhoods pillar.

files.title: Prototype Recommendations
files.summary: Synthesized prototype recommendations for the most viable Thriving Neighborhoods build directions.

files.title: Continuation Plan
files.summary: Post-hackathon continuation plan identifying potential partners, next steps, and artifacts worth pursuing beyond the weekend.

files.title: MVP Shapes
files.summary: Describes the main MVP shapes available to Thriving Neighborhoods teams, with scope, data requirements, and tradeoffs for each.

files.title: Recommended Architectures
files.summary: Technical architecture recommendations for the most viable Thriving Neighborhoods MVPs.

files.title: Demo Advice
files.summary: Practical advice for preparing and delivering the hackathon demo for Thriving Neighborhoods projects.

files.title: Skill: repo_memory
files.summary: Reconstruct repository state — reads project artifacts, detects stage of work, summarizes progress.

files.title: Skill: problem_scoping
files.summary: Help teams choose a strong problem — analyzes problem statements, ranks opportunities, identifies realistic directions.

files.title: Skill: research_runner
files.summary: Execute research prompts and collect findings — runs prompts, extracts sources, summarizes evidence.

files.title: Skill: dataset_mapper
files.summary: Identify usable datasets — scans data directory, identifies schemas, highlights gaps.

files.title: Skill: opportunity_mapper
files.summary: Translate research into buildable solution opportunities — matches problems to solution patterns, suggests MVP directions.

files.title: Skill: mvp_designer
files.summary: Turn an opportunity into a concrete MVP — outputs user flow, architecture, and scope boundaries.

files.title: Skill: risk_review
files.summary: Prevent civic-tech mistakes — checks for hallucinated services, legal claims, incorrect eligibility logic, and misleading language.

files.title: Skill: demo_coach
files.summary: Help teams present effectively — outputs demo script, narrative structure, judge-facing explanation.

files.title: Skill: repo_librarian
files.summary: Help users navigate repository knowledge — answers questions about where to find information in the repo.

files.title: Skill: continuity_planner
files.summary: Identify post-hackathon continuation opportunities — outputs potential partners, artifacts worth continuing, next steps.

files.title: Skill: research_search
files.summary: Search and retrieve relevant research content from the corpus based on a user query.

files.title: Skill: research_corpus_navigation
files.summary: Map a user question to the correct section and specific research reports before attempting to answer.

files.title: Skill: cross_report_synthesis
files.summary: Synthesize findings from multiple research reports into a coherent answer while preserving source traceability and acknowledging uncertainty.

files.title: Skill: evidence_grounded_answering
files.summary: Answer user questions using only content actually present in this repository corpus, with explicit grounding and uncertainty marking.

files.title: Skill: report_update_protocol
files.summary: Safely add or update research reports and keep all metadata, indexes, and navigation files synchronized.

files.title: Project One-Pager Template
files.summary: Template for creating a one-page project brief after scope lock.

files.title: Team Profile Template
files.summary: Template for creating a team profile file that Hackbot uses to personalize assistance.

files.title: Working Direction Note Template
files.summary: Template for capturing the team's working problem direction during orientation.

files.title: Decision Memo Template
files.summary: Template for recording the team's chosen problem, user, rationale, and risks after problem selection.

files.title: Research Corpus Index (JSON)
files.summary: Machine-readable index of all 51 research reports with section, title, summary, and key_terms for each. See individual entries in research/index.json.
